name: Linus Torvalds
title: Fellow at Linux Foundation
aboutMeExpanded:
As a visionary systems architect and open-source pioneer, my career has been defined by creating foundational technologies that power the modern digital world. Serving as a Fellow at the Linux Foundation, I have dedicated decades to stewarding the global open-source ecosystem and guiding the collaborative development of software that runs on billions of devices, from tiny embedded sensors to the world's largest supercomputers. My work focuses on establishing robust, scalable, and highly performant standards that empower developers worldwide to build without boundaries.

My technical expertise lies at the intersection of low-level systems programming, operating system kernel architecture, and distributed version control systems. I possess a deep-seated passion for writing clean, highly optimized C code and designing architectures that prioritize efficiency, security, and hardware compatibility. Throughout my journey, I have championed pragmatic engineering philosophies, demonstrating that elegant code must also be robust enough to withstand the demands of massive, real-world production environments.

Beyond core kernel development, I am continuously driven by curiosity to explore hardware-software interfaces, embedded systems, and innovative developer tooling. I thrive on solving complex, low-level engineering challenges, whether that involves optimizing memory latency, interfacing with custom microcontrollers, or designing intuitive command-line utilities. I remain committed to mentoring the next generation of engineers and fostering open, collaborative communities that push the boundaries of what technology can achieve.

featuredProjects.title: linux
featuredProjects.summary: Developed in C, this industry-defining repository contains the complete source tree for the Linux kernel, serving as the foundational operating system core for modern cloud infrastructure, supercomputers, and mobile devices. Managing this codebase involves coordinating massive distributed contributions while maintaining extreme performance, hardware compatibility, and low-level system security.

featuredProjects.title: AudioNoise
featuredProjects.summary: This project leverages C to implement a suite of random digital audio effects, showcasing real-time signal processing capabilities. The complexity lies in optimizing low-latency mathematical algorithms to generate and manipulate audio streams efficiently at the system level.

featuredProjects.title: uemacs
featuredProjects.summary: Written in C, this repository features a customized, highly optimized version of the MicroEMACS text editor tailored with private modifications for streamlined developer workflows. It demonstrates deep system-level customization, focusing on lightweight memory footprints and rapid keyboard-driven text manipulation.

standardProjects.title: ScrollWheel
standardProjects.summary: Built using C, this embedded systems project implements a minimalist scroll wheel interface utilizing a magnetic sensor on the RP2350 microcontroller. It showcases low-level hardware integration, precise sensor calibration, and real-time input processing on resource-constrained microcontrollers.

standardProjects.title: pesconvert
standardProjects.summary: This utility, written in C, is designed to parse and convert proprietary Brother PES embroidery file formats into standard vector representations. The project requires reverse-engineering binary file structures and implementing precise coordinate mapping algorithms to ensure accurate design translation.

standardProjects.title: libgit2
standardProjects.summary: This highly complex, cross-platform library written in C provides a linkable implementation of Git core methods for integration into external applications. It demands rigorous memory management, thread safety, and deep compatibility with diverse operating system file systems to ensure reliable version control operations.

standardProjects.title: test-tlb
standardProjects.summary: Developed in C, this diagnostic tool measures memory latency and tests Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB) performance to analyze hardware bottlenecks. The complexity involves writing precise, low-level assembly-adjacent code to bypass caching layers and accurately benchmark CPU memory management units.

standardProjects.title: GuitarPedal
standardProjects.summary: This project documents the exploration of analog circuitry and digital signal processing, utilizing C to program embedded audio effects for custom guitar hardware. It bridges the gap between physical electrical engineering and low-level firmware development to achieve real-time analog-to-digital audio manipulation.

standardProjects.title: HunspellColorize
standardProjects.summary: Written in C, this utility acts as a terminal wrapper around the 'less' pager to dynamically colorize spelling mistakes using the Hunspell library. It involves parsing terminal escape sequences and managing real-time text stream processing to deliver an interactive, visual command-line tool.

experience.description: Previous name for the Linux Foundation, before the merger with LSB

customSections.content: Main Linux kernel developer since 1991, still active maintainer. Original architect and author of the 'Git' source control management system, still active in development but no longer the primary maintainer. Specialties: System programming - Linux and git
